Mr. Mr. Khalid Mehmood Siddiqui, Advocate Syed Mohsin Ali Advocate Mr. Naeem Suleman, Advocate Mr. Ashraf Shahzad, Advocate. Mr. Liaqat Ali Abro, Consultant to Chief Secretary Sindh. Mr. Ali Asadullah Bullo, Advocate Date of hearing & order: 27.02.2023. == MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Petitioners, private Ltd. companies have challenged impugned orders passed by respondent No.1 (EOBI) rejecting their appeals and assuming jurisdiction under provisions of Employees Old Age Benefits Act, 1976.

Mr. Ali Asadullah Bullo, Advocate Date of hearing & order: 27.02.2023. == MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Petitioners, private Ltd. companies have challenged impugned orders passed by respondent No.1 (EOBI) rejecting their appeals and assuming jurisdiction under provisions of Employees Old Age Benefits Act, 1976. They have challenged the jurisdiction of the EOBI on the ground that Act,1976 stands lapsed w.e.f 30.06.2011 in the backdrop of 18th Amendment to the Constitution of Pakistan, 1973. 2. Learned Assistant Attorney General and learned counsel for respondents submit that the present petitions and issues involved are duly covered by the order dated 22.02.2023 of Division Bench of this court passed in C.P.No.D-7347 of 2022 & others) and may be disposed of accordingly. 3. We have heard the parties and are of the same view postulated by learned Assistant Attorney General and counsel for respondents and therefore, dispose of these present petitions mutatis mutandis in the terms of the aforesaid order passed in C.P.No.D-7347 of 2022 M/s Matco Foods Ltd. Vs. Federation of Pakistan & Others. The petitions are disposed of alongwith pending application(s) accordingly. Office is directed to place copy of this order in the connected petitions as mentioned above. JUDGE JUDGE A.K.
